title: Flank Pain - Ureteral Stone
chief_complaint: 40-year-old male with acute severe left flank pain radiating to groin for 4 hours. Nausea, vomiting. Cannot find comfortable position. Hematuria noted.

physical_exam.abdomen: Left flank tenderness, no rebound, no masses

hidden_data.ct_kub: 6mm stone at left UVJ (ureterovesical junction) with mild hydronephrosis

correct_diagnosis: Left Ureteral Stone (6mm at UVJ) with Hydronephrosis
key_learning_points: Flank to groin pain + hematuria = stone until proven
Pain control: NSAIDs first-line, opioids if needed

patient_responses.pain: It comes in waves, worst pain of my life
patient_responses.location: Starts in my back and goes to my groin
patient_responses.urine: There was blood in my urine

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Ureteral stone at UVJ.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is a ureteral stone. The flank pain + hematuria + CT confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: 6mm UVJ stone: Medical expulsive therapy with tamsulosin 0.4mg daily. NSAIDs for pain. >90% pass spontaneously. Strain urine. Follow-up US in 2 weeks. Ureteroscopy if fails to pass in 4 weeks or obstructive symptoms worsen.
